text: David. He, (Calmet) Ezechias, (Ven. Bede) the captives, (Bossuet) or Jesus Christ and his servants under persecution, speak in this psalm. (Holy Fathers) See Psalm lv. (Calmet)
Man. Saul, (Calmet) Judas, (St. Hilary) or self-love, the old man, who is our most dangerous enemy. (Berthier)
Battles. And formed plots to destroy the just. (Worthington)
Serpent. So Plautus says, "A double-tongued and wicked man is like a creeping beast," in Persa. (Haydock)
Side. The enemies of David calumniated him, and strove to make him follow idols, 1 Kings 26:19.
Battle. Against Goliath, or against the attempts of Saul, etc. (Calmet)<hr>Prayer and divine grace are necessary, to guard us from sin. (Worthington)

Desire. If I yield to my passions, (St. Augustine) or after I have expressed my request, contrary to my desire, etc. Hebrew, "Grant not the desire of the sinner, complete not his thought. They will be elated." Some supply, "lest they be." The ancients seem not to have read in the same manner. (Berthier)<hr>Abandon not my soul, which is all my care, (Psalm 21:21.) to the sinner. (Calmet)
Head. Hebrew also, "poison," (Berthier) or "sum" of their mischievous devices. (Worthington)<hr>Labour, or punishment. (Calmet)<hr>Their subtle persuasions shall turn to their ruin. (Worthington)

Fire of hell, (Worthington; Chaldean) as well as temporal afflictions, from lightning, etc., Psalm 17:9. (Haydock)<hr>Some have ridiculed the belief of hell. But the universality of this opinion is a strong proof of it, as it is also confirmed by revelation. (Berthier)

Tongue. This member is very dangerous. (Pet. Bless. cxix.) (James 3:6.)<hr>The just man is not styled "a man of tongue," but "of heart;" for which reason Christ exhorted his disciples not to make long speeches in prayer, Matthew 6:7. (Berthier)<hr>Into. Literally, "in;" though (Haydock) it should be into, conformably to the Septuagint. (Berthier) Raro antecedentem scelestum Deseruit pede poena claudo. (Horace, 3:Od. ii.)
I know. Hebrew, "thou knowest." But some copies read more accurately. (Houbigant)<hr>Poor. Thus, in the end, was Lazarus treated. [Luke 16:22.] (Worthington)
Countenance in glory, when thou shalt appear, 1 John 3:2. (Haydock)<hr>They shall dwell under thy special protection in the tabernacle. (Calmet)
